channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Japan Raises Rates to 31-Year High to Ward Off War Inflation
item.description: Going against Prime Minister Takaichi’s wishes, Japan’s central bank acted amid U.S. pressure, a tanking currency and inflation from energy disruptions.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis and Kiuko Notoya
item.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan in Tokyo last month. Her government spending agenda faces pressure from rising interest rates.

item.title: China’s Spending Slowdown Deepens as Households Tighten Their Belts
item.description: The unexpected decline in retail sales underscored China’s growing reliance on exports to sustain economic growth as domestic demand continues to falter.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Retail sales dropped 0.6 percent in May from the same month a year earlier, the National Bureau of Statistics said on Tuesday.

item.title: How Kratom, an Addictive Gas Station Drug, Found Allies in Trump’s Cabinet
item.description: With support from Markwayne Mullin and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the kratom industry is pursuing a potentially lucrative policy. Mr. Mullin owns equity in a company that could benefit.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel and Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Nick Oxford for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bottles of Feel Free, a kratom product produced by Botanic Tonics, displayed at a smoke shop in Oklahoma City last month.

item.title: Will SpaceX Fortunes Trickle Down to This Texas Town?
item.description: Elon Musk built a huge complex outside the city of Bastrop, population 14,000. Its residents now wonder what the historic SpaceX initial public offering might mean for them.
item.dc:creator: David Segal
item.media:credit: Montinique Monroe for The New York Times
item.media:description: North Main Street in downtown Bastrop, Texas. The town’s population has boomed since Elon Musk built a complex of his companies nearby.

item.title: Deal to Reopen Hormuz Kicks Off Long Effort to Ease Energy Crisis
item.description: The pace of the recovery will depend on how confident companies are that the deal between the United States and Iran will hold and be extended.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz this month.

item.title: SpaceX’s Stock Surges on First Full Day of Trading
item.description: The company’s shares jumped on Monday, and executives announced that they had raised even more money in an already record-breaking initial offering.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McCarthy, Ryan Mac and Joe Rennison
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
item.media:description: SpaceX executives celebrated the company’s market debut at the Nasdaq on Friday after the largest initial public offering in history.

item.title: Fox to Acquire Roku, Joining the Battle for the Living Room
item.description: The $22 billion deal is one of the biggest moves yet by Lachlan Murdoch, who took over for his father as chairman of Fox in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:description: Roku’s headquarters in San Jose, Calif. Fox’s acquisition of Roku will give it a piece of the infrastructure that powers a major chunk of the streaming business.

item.title: Global Markets Rally on Iran Peace Hopes
item.description: A framework agreement could be signed later this week, but details are scarce. Still, investors are cheering the potential breakthrough.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Vivienne Walt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Hopes that the Strait of Hormuz will fully reopen have prompted a global market rally.

item.title: Cautious of U.S.-Iran Deal, Shipping Firms Await Strait to Reopen
item.description: Shipowners welcomed the agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but said they would need more security guarantees before resuming transits.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Monday.

item.title: What to Know About Planned Social Media Bans in Australia, the U.K. and Beyond
item.description: Britain said it would ban social media access for children under 16 starting in 2027, joining several other countries introducing similar measures.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:description: Students in Wimbledon, London, during an interview this year about social media. Public opinion in many countries has coalesced around the idea that more must be done to protect children online.

item.title: China Moves the Price of Oil, Even When It Buys Less
item.description: China is the world’s largest purchaser of oil. But three months after the war with Iran began, it cut its imports, cushioning the global market.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman and Alexandra Stevenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil refinery in Nanjing in China’s Jiangsu Province.

item.title: To Toast America’s Birthday, They Blended Bourbon From Every State
item.description: United States of Bourbon, the result of a Vermont couple’s many road trips, is just one of several special whiskeys marking the 250th.
item.dc:creator: Liza Weisstuch
item.media:credit: Oliver Parini for The New York Times

item.title: Taking on Neo-Nazis, One Trademark at a Time
item.description: In Germany, a campaign called Rights Against the Right is securing trademarks for right-wing symbols and phrases, aiming to cut into a revenue stream that sustains hate groups.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:credit: Schore Mehrdju for The New York Times
item.media:description: Among the latest trademarks awarded to Rights Against was for the term “ess ess,” code for S.S., or Schutzstaffel, the elite guard of the Nazi Reich.

item.title: UK Announces Social Media Ban for Children Under 16
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said his government planned to bar children under 16 from social media, following similar efforts in Australia and elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: High school students in Wimbledon, London, this year during an interview about social media. Britain plans to place an age limit on social media.

item.title: Hormuz Reopening Would Offer Relief for Asia, but Economic Scars Will Remain
item.description: For months, Asia has suffered a physical supply crunch that will likely drag on its economies long after the crucial waterway reopens.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz in Oman last week. More than four-fifths of the petroleum and liquefied natural gas transiting the strait is typically bound for Asian markets.

item.title: Stocks Surge as U.S. and Iran Reach Preliminary Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices fell and stocks gained early Monday after Iran and the U.S. reached a deal that may allow more energy and other goods to flow through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Carolyn Kaster/Associated Press, via Ap Photo/Carolyn Kaster

item.title: Jean Ziegler, Swiss Gadfly Who Provoked His Countrymen, Dies at 92
item.description: In a nation that sees itself as a tranquil oasis of prosperity and business virtue, he drew death threats for pointing out a dark underside.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Swiss, the Gold, and the Dead, The (Book)
item.media:credit: Michael Gottschalk/DDP, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Jean Ziegler in 2009. A writer, sociologist and politician, he was called Switzerland’s “national troublemaker” by Le Monde in 1997 for his critiques of Swiss society, particularly the banking system.

item.title: ‘Disclosure Day’ Ends Spielberg’s Summer Box Office Drought
item.description: Steven Spielberg’s original science-fiction movie collected an estimated $44 million in North America, giving the director his first new-to-the-screen summer hit in 24 years.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.media:credit: Roy Rochlin/Getty Images for Universal Pictures
item.media:description: Universal Pictures’ promotional campaign for “Disclosure Day” sought to remind summertime audiences that Steven Spielberg was capable of delivering a major event movie.

item.title: Merger Cleared, David Ellison to Join Trump at U.F.C. Bouts
item.description: A U.F.C. fight on President Trump’s birthday will now double as a capstone for Paramount’s successful effort to secure Justice Department approval for a mega media merger.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:description: The specially constructed Claw on the South Lawn of the White House, where the Ultimate Fighting Championship bouts will be held on Sunday.

item.title: The G7 Summit Is Dogged by Chaos and Divided by Trump
item.description: Group of 7 meetings once embodied the effort to sustain the global diplomatic order. This year’s gathering, starting on Monday, symbolizes its fragmentation.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Leaders of the Group of 7 nations at a summit in Kananaskis, Canada, last year.

item.title: How to Run a News Company in the Age of Polarization and A.I. Slop
item.description: Cesar Conde of NBCUniversal News thinks that the “pendulum will eventually swing back” to mainstream media brands.
item.dc:creator: Jordyn Holman
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cesar Conde has been the chairman NBC’s news unit since 2020, and was previously credited with turning around the fortunes of Telemundo.

item.title: Kash Patel Keeps Suing the Press
item.description: The F.B.I. director, following a strategy from President Trump, has filed six defamation lawsuits against news media companies and commentators in nearly seven years.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Kash Patel, director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, has yet to reach a settlement or a favorable jury verdict from the cases.

item.title: Medicare 101: What You Need to Know
item.description: The federal health insurance program can be complicated. Here’s how to start, and where to find help.
item.dc:creator: Mark Miller
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services

item.title: Everyone Wants to Tax A.I. The Big Disagreement: How?
item.description: Bernie Sanders, President Trump and even A.I. companies say they would like the public to share the wealth. But their solutions are very different.
item.dc:creator: Peter Coy

item.title: Wages Are Falling. Wealth Is Surging. No Wonder Americans Are Unhappy.
item.description: As Elon Musk became the world’s first trillionaire, workers are facing higher prices and fears of A.I.-driven job losses.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Times Square on Friday, when Elon Musk’s SpaceX went public on the Nasdaq exchange.

item.title: Mega I.P.O. Frenzy Could Be a Harbinger of a Stock Bubble
item.description: Rampant enthusiasm is buoying tech shares to levels that defy gravity. Invest with caution, our columnist says.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer

item.title: Despite US Help, Little Oil Has Gone Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said more than 200 commercial vessels had safely traveled through the strait. That’s still far fewer than before the start of the war.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Global stockpiles of oil continue to decline as vessels remain stuck, unable to transit the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: At the Canadian Screen Awards, the Industry Steps Outside Hollywood’s Shadow
item.description: No longer content with being just “Hollywood North,” the Canadian screen industry is asserting itself and telling its own stories.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff and Cole Burston
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actor Robbie Graham-Kunz speaking with fans outside the 14th Canadian Screen Awards in Toronto.

item.title: Musk Is the World’s First Trillionaire. Who Was the First Billionaire?
item.description: John D. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became America’s first billionaire in 1916. Like Elon Musk’s ascension, the milestone made headlines.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:description: John D. Rockefeller Sr., in Lakewood, N.J., in 1933. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became a billionaire in 1916.

item.title: Justice Dept. Clears Way for Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
item.description: The $111 billion deal would unite two major movie studios and put CNN under the same roof as CBS News.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch, David McCabe and Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery would include the Warner Bros. movie studio, above, in Burbank, Calif.

item.title: SpaceX’s Unlikely Journey From Far-Out Idea to $2 Trillion Juggernaut
item.description: Elon Musk said he had initially given SpaceX less than a 10 percent chance of succeeding. His rocket company has come a long way.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Kenneth Chang and Kirsten Grind
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
item.media:description: A SpaceX ad on the Nasdaq MarketSite tower in Times Square. The company, which Elon Musk founded in 2002, began trading on the stock market on Friday.

item.title: The steady hand at SpaceX is not Elon Musk’s.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: Gwynne Shotwell, SpaceX’s president and chief operating officer, in Miami Beach last year during one of her infrequent public appearances. “I love working for Elon,” she has said.

item.title: SpaceX is structured in a way that appears to benefit Musk at the expense of other shareholders.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: Central to Elon Musk’s power over SpaceX is his control of “super voting” shares.

item.title: Elon Musk laid out 602 goals. We counted how many he hit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grind, Jack Ewing, Aaron Krolik and Lily Boyce

item.title: Elon Musk’s Big Bet for SpaceX
item.description: Elon Musk is taking SpaceX public and keeping almost all the power. Ryan Mac, a technology reporter for The Times, breaks down why the company’s record I.P.O. is both a big bet on Musk’s vision and a risk for investors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Sam Rosenthal

item.title: If You Have a 401(k), How Much SpaceX Stock Will You Own?
item.description: If you have index funds — such as in a 401(k) — they may soon include shares of the mega company. Here’s a rough calculation.
item.dc:creator: C.J. Robinson

item.title: Musk posted about politics, race and the culture wars, even as he took SpaceX public.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger
item.media:description: Elon Musk at the Kennedy Center in Washington, in 2025.

item.title: SpaceX is about to be in your 401(k) whether you like it or not.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac and Maureen Farrell
item.media:description: SpaceX initial public offering signage is displayed at the Bank of America building in New York.

item.title: About 20 New Billionaires Could Be Minted by 3 Mega-I.P.O.s
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI could join SpaceX with their own initial public offerings, setting many employees up to become millionaires or billionaires.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dario Amodei, the co-founder and chief executive of Anthropic, in December.

item.title: Blake Lively Awarded Legal Fees in Ruling After Justin Baldoni Settlement
item.description: The size of the award remains to be determined. It only involves the legal fees associated with part of her court battle with Mr. Baldoni, with whom she made a film.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: A judge on Friday awarded Blake Lively some legal fees in her court battle with Justin Baldoni. The amount was not set in the ruling.

item.title: Elon Musk Becomes World’s First Trillionaire as SpaceX Stock Begins Trading
item.description: With SpaceX shares soaring 20 percent on their first day of trading, the world’s richest person crossed another milestone — one with 13 digits.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Ben Casselman
item.media:description: Elon Musk, the chief executive of SpaceX, in 2022 at the company’s facility in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: SpaceX Stock Rises 11% in Largest IPO Ever
item.description: The blockbuster stock market debut of Elon Musk’s rocket company paves the way for a wave of enormous offerings that are coming from OpenAI and Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Traders at the New York Stock Exchange watching news about SpaceX’s initial public offering on the Nasdaq exchange on Friday.

item.title: Is SpaceX worth $1.77 trillion? It’s a pie in the sky, some investors say.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac
item.media:description: SpaceX set an initial public offering price of $135 a share, valuing it at $1.77 trillion. Some investors are skeptical that the valuation is justified.

item.title: The World Cup Could Be the Biggest Sports Gambling Event Ever
item.description: At a time of sluggish growth, betting sites like FanDuel and DraftKings are counting on the global soccer tournament to boost online sports gambling.
item.dc:creator: Joe Drape

item.title: When will SpaceX start trading?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: SpaceX advertisements in Times Square on Friday morning, ahead of its stock’s debut trading.

item.title: Americans Aren’t Money Savvy, and They’re Only Getting Worse
item.description: Researchers say people with poor understanding of basic finances often make bad decisions on money matters.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch
